title: Guy of Warwick (Later Couplet version)
summary: The General Corpus XML is a normalized copy of the University of Michigan CME encoding ANZ4364.0001.001, keyed from a complete 482-TIFF capture of Julius Zupitza's 1875-1876 EETS edition. Zupitza transcribes Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38 as principal text and collates only two exact line ranges against Gonville and Caius College MS 107/176. Ff.2.38 contains the complete 11,976-line Later Couplet redaction, including the integral Harrawde/Reinbrun continuation. Complete U-M package and independent Zupitza edition scans are cached outside Git; no verified public complete digital facsimile of Ff.2.38 or Caius MS 107/176 was found, and a CUL whole-manuscript reproduction enquiry is pending.
review_notes: Primary transmission relation IDs: relation:artifact-copied-from-raw-umich; relation:raw-umich-encoded-from-tiffs; relation:umich-tiffs-facsimile-of-zupitza; relation:zupitza-transcribes-ff238; relation:ff238-contains-later-couplet; relation:later-couplet-version-of-gui.

Supporting relation IDs: relation:ia-package-contains-raw; relation:ia-package-contains-tiffs; relation:ia-scan-facsimile-of-zupitza; relation:ia-ocr-encoded-from-scan; relation:zupitza-contains-later-couplet; relation:zupitza-collated-against-caius; relation:caius-contains-later-couplet-passages; relation:mcsparran-facsimile-of-ff238; relation:dimev-describes-work; relation:dimev-describes-ff238; relation:dimev-describes-caius; relation:dmer-describes-work; relation:dmer-describes-ff238; relation:cul1857-describes-ff238; relation:wiggins-describes-work; relation:wiggins-describes-ff238; relation:wiggins-describes-zupitza; relation:dixon-describes-ff238.
The cataloging boundary is one 11,976-line work. Guy lines 1-10,786 and the Harrawde/Reinbrun continuation lines 10,787-11,976 are analytical movements, not separately counted works.
Printed p.310 is shared by the two analytical movements. In Ff.2.38, Guy ends on f.231ra, f.231rb is blank, and the continuation begins on f.231va.
DIMEV record 4908 gives a whole-redaction extent but a Guy-only terminal locator; D-MER likewise omits the continuation from its Ff.2.38 item list. Wiggins and Zupitza control the complete boundary.

Access and rights attach to named layers. Public-domain medieval text does not automatically confer reuse rights in modern photographs, interfaces, encodings, scholarship, or future CUL-supplied files.

Carrier-mediated rights access stays on the same entity as the rights record: the raw encoding is reached through artifact:internet-archive:anz4364-umich-source-package, and the Zupitza edition is reached through facsimile:internet-archive:zupitza-1875-1876.

Historical publication from 1857; provider scan remains separately attributable.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Ff.2.38 principal text with two exact Caius comparison ranges
editorial_practices.representation: Ff.2.38 supplies the base; Caius corrects or points obvious faults only in the scoped ranges
editorial_practices.description: Ff.2.38 supplies the principal text. Caius MS 107/176 is used only at lines 7,261-8,024 and 8,541-9,874.
editorial_practices.scope: Lines 1-11,976, with Caius comparison restricted to lines 7,261-8,024 and 8,541-9,874

editorial_practices.description: Zupitza supplies punctuation because the manuscript is largely unpunctuated.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Manuscript capitalization, i/j usage, and compound-word joining
editorial_practices.representation: Regulated capitalization, i/j, and compound-word joining
editorial_practices.description: Zupitza regulates capitalization, i/j, and compound-word joining.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Departures from Ff.2.38 and minor scribal features
editorial_practices.representation: Substantive departures reported in footnotes; a small class of trifles silently normalized
editorial_practices.description: Substantive departures from Ff.2.38 are reported in footnotes; a small class of trifles is silently normalized.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Large initials mark manuscript divisions; only three paragraph marks are reported before lines 7,487, 11,267, and 11,337
editorial_practices.representation: Large initials retained as division cues; editor adds paragraph marks for shorter divisions
editorial_practices.description: Large initials mark manuscript divisions. The editor adds paragraph marks for shorter divisions, while only three paragraph marks are said to occur in the manuscript before lines 7,487, 11,267, and 11,337.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Shared intellectual-movement page boundary and two-part EETS serial publication
editorial_practices.representation: Both analytical parts include printed p.310; the two EETS serial numbers are recorded without an invented exact split
editorial_practices.description: Printed p.310 contains both the four-line Guy ending and the opening of the Harrawde/Reinbrun continuation. The edition was issued as EETS Extra Series numbers 25 and 26 in 1875-1876, but the exact original part/page boundary was not verified from the bound scan.
editorial_practices.scope: Analytical movement boundary on printed p.310 and bibliographic serial-part description
notes: Keep the movements' line and manuscript-column locators distinct despite their shared printed page.
